payload.author: agent
payload.body:
Delivered. trust_std::newtype! makes the R0017 fix a one-liner.

- trust-std: #[macro_export] macro_rules! newtype — 'newtype!(pub Width(u32));' generates a tuple struct with a pub field + new/into_inner + From<inner>, deriving Debug/Clone/PartialEq (safe for any inner incl f64). Richer derives (Copy/Eq/Hash/Ord) via an extra #[derive(...)] before the decl (meta passthrough). 3 lib tests + 2 doc-tests.
- R0017's help text now points at it; SPEC R0017 + std-shims sections document it.
- Proven end-to-end: raw u32 params fire R0017; the newtype! fix silences it (distinct types) with no R0008 (invocation, not a macro_rules def).

Home: trust-std (proc-macro crates can't export macro_rules; needs a normal lib). It's a macro_rules DEF in a strict crate, so 'trust check crates/trust-std' reports R0008 on it — but the wrapper runs lowering only (not AST lints), so this is NOT CI-enforced, consistent with R0017's own trust-std std-mirror hits. Verified: stock build/test/clippy -D warnings, dogfood wrapper build of trust-std, and gen-docs/gen-std-signatures --check all green.
